briefdescription.para: Gets unique address on accessibility bus.
Gets index that current object has in its parent's children collection.

simplesect.para: The index of the current object

briefdescription.para: Gets the Actor associated with this Accessible (if there is one).

simplesect.para: The string property value. Empty string if property does not exist.

briefdescription.para: Gets layer current object is localized on.

simplesect.para: MDI means "Multi Document Interface" (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Multiple-document_interface) which in short means that many stacked windows can be displayed within a single application. In such model, the concept of z-order of UI element became important to deal with element overlapping.
The alpha channel value in range [0.0, 1.0]

briefdescription.para: Checks whether object can be scrolled.

simplesect.para: true if object is scrollable, false otherwise

parameterdescription.para: The enumeration with type of coordinate systems

memberdef.argsstring: (MatchRule rule, uint32_t sortBy, size_t maxCount) override
memberdef.name: GetMatches

briefdescription.para: Gets the matching Accessible objects with MatchRule.

parameterdescription.para: The maximum number of objects; returns all matches if 0

memberdef.argsstring: (MatchRule firstRule, MatchRule secondRule, uint32_t sortBy, int32_t firstCount, int32_t secondCount) override
memberdef.name: GetMatchesInMatches

briefdescription.para: Gets the matching Accessible objects with two MatchRules.

parameterdescription.para: The maximum number of objects to return for the initial match.; returns all matches if 0
The maximum number of objects to return for the secondary match.; returns all matches if 0

briefdescription.para: Notifies this object that its children have changed.

detaileddescription.para: This is useful if you maintain a custom collection of children that are not derived from ActorAccessible and the contents or order of elements in that collection change.

briefdescription.para: Helper function for emiting active-descendant-changed event.
Helper function for emiting state-changed event.

parameterdescription.para: Whether the state value is changed to new value or not.
Reserved. (TODO : Currently, this argument is not implemented in dali)

simplesect.para: The second argument determines which value is depending on State. For instance, if the state is PRESSED, newValue means isPressed or isSelected. If the state is SHOWING, newValue means isShowing.

briefdescription.para: Helper function for emiting bounds-changed event.
Emits "showing" event. The method informs accessibility clients about "showing" state.

parameterdescription.para: The flag pointing if object is showing

briefdescription.para: Emits "visible" event. The method informs accessibility clients about "visible" state.

parameterdescription.para: The flag pointing if object is visible

briefdescription.para: Emits "highlighted" event. The method informs accessibility clients about "highlighted" state.

parameterdescription.para: The flag pointing if object is highlighted

briefdescription.para: Emits "focused" event. The method informs accessibility clients about "focused" state.

parameterdescription.para: The flag pointing if object is focused

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned int position, unsigned int length, const std::string &content)
memberdef.name: EmitTextInserted

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned int position, unsigned int length, const std::string &content)
memberdef.name: EmitTextDeleted

parameterdescription.para: The additional parameter which interpretation depends on chosen event

briefdescription.para: Re-emits selected states of an Accessibility Object.

parameterdescription.para: If true, all children of the Accessibility object will also re-emit the states

briefdescription.para: Clears internal cache data on bridge down.
Checks if the accessible object can be accepted even with zero size.

detaileddescription.para: This pure virtual function must be implemented by derived classes to initialize the default set of accessibility features for the specific accessible object type.

simplesect.para: This function is called during the initialization of the accessible object to set up the basic accessibility features that are always available.

briefdescription.para: Populates the collection of children of this Accessible.

detaileddescription.para: The default implementation retrieves the children from the Actor hierarchy. Override this if you want to report other objects as children, either instead of, or together with the dependent Actor-derived Accessibles. Remember to call OnChildrenChanged() if you want your implementation of DoGetChildren() to be called again (in case your custom collection of children changes).

parameterdescription.para: The initially empty vector to insert children into

simplesect.para: GetChildCound(), GetChildren() and GetChildAtIndex() are not available for overriding, but they respect the children collection reported by this method.
